Comparing the before and after states:
- id=0 (pink timer): pixels changed from 63 to 61 (decreased by 2).
- id=1 (charcoal): pixels changed from 1 to 3, boundary changed from [[0, 63]] to [[0, 61], [0, 63]]...
So the only change is the timer bar (pink) decreased by 2 pixels, and a small charcoal object at the top changed. The gameplay objects (charcoal vertical bar, yellow bar, blue blocks, etc.) did not change.
This means clicking on the charcoal vertical bar only affected the timer, not the gameplay objects. This is similar to clicking on the charcoal L-shape earlier.
So clicking on the charcoal shape (whether L-shape or vertical bar) only affects the timer. This is not useful.
